introSection: Wild One was a wooden roller coaster formerly located at Six Flags America in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, USA. It was relocated from Paragon Park in Hull, Massachusetts, USA, where it stood as Giant Coaster from 1917 to 1985.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Wild World purchased the roller coaster from Paragon Park for $28,000 at auction. Charles Dinn oversaw the transportation and reconstruction of the roller coaster, which took 16 trucks. As part of the project, a 540-degree helix was added to the end of the ride, increasing its length from to. It reopened as Wild One in 1986.

Wild One closed permanently on November 2, 2025, along with Six Flags America.

fullText:
Wild One was a wooden roller coaster formerly located at Six Flags America in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, USA. It was relocated from Paragon Park in Hull, Massachusetts, USA, where it stood as Giant Coaster from 1917 to 1985.

Wild World purchased the roller coaster from Paragon Park for $28,000 at auction. Charles Dinn oversaw the transportation and reconstruction of the roller coaster, which took 16 trucks. As part of the project, a 540-degree helix was added to the end of the ride, increasing its length from to. It reopened as Wild One in 1986.

Wild One closed permanently on November 2, 2025, along with Six Flags America.



Unpainted track and white supports.
